title: Report: Celtics’ Paul George turned down $3.9 million trade bonus
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Paul George waived a $3.9 million trade bonus, maintaining a $54.1 million cap hit with the Celtics.
Boston Celtics remain $30 million over the salary cap, with $25 million space before the first luxury tax apron.
The Celtics prioritize avoiding severe penalties linked to the second luxury tax apron by managing their salary cap.

title: R.I. food bank thanks Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce for pre-wedding donation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donated $1 million to the Rhode Island Community Food Bank before their wedding.
The donation supports 137 member agencies, addressing increased food needs with nutritious and culturally appropriate options.
The couple's contributions extend beyond Rhode Island, donating to multiple charities including Feeding America and Helping Harvest.

title: 5 takeaways from the Celtics’ press conference about the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia for Paul George and draft assets, focusing on financial and strategic adjustments.
Brad Stevens emphasized the trade's necessity due to new CBA rules affecting salary cap distribution for star players.
Jaylen Brown expressed feeling disrespected by trade discussions, despite significant contributions to Celtics over ten seasons.

title: US vs. Belgium live coverage: Folarin Balogun in starting lineup for World Cup Round of 32
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The US soccer team faces Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16 tonight at 8 p.m.
Folarin Balogun is starting for the US after FIFA controversially suspended his red card.
Belgium's lineup omits Kevin De Bruyne and Jeremy Doku, surprising pregame analysts.

title: 2 siblings drown in Vermont river, brother tried to save younger sister, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sandro Lala, 25, and Yazmin Yupangui, 11, drowned in Vermont's Lamoille River last week, police confirmed.
Yazmin fell into the river; Sandro attempted rescue but both disappeared, prompting a search operation.
Following challenging river conditions, Yazmin's body was found Thursday and Sandro's on Friday, both ruled accidental drownings.

title: City declines to use reserve funds to prevent BPS layoffs as more than 560 positions are cut
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: More than 560 positions in Boston Public Schools are cut following approval of a $1.7 billion budget.
Boston Mayor Michelle Wu decides against using reserve funds to prevent Boston Public Schools layoffs.
Enrollment declines and a net drop in state aid contribute to Boston's decision to reduce BPS staff.

title: Harmful bacteria persist in Merrimack River days after Haverhill sewer break
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Testing shows harmful bacteria levels remain high in Merrimack River after a major sewer spill in Haverhill.
Emergency bypass installed, but four out of seven river sites still exceed safe bacteria limits; beaches impacted.
Shellfish areas in Gloucester, Newburyport, and others remain closed; increased sampling by marine authorities ongoing.

title: Boater remains missing after crash off of Salisbury Beach
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A boater remains missing after a crash into the jetty near Salisbury Beach late Friday night.
Two individuals were rescued and hospitalized after the crash, but Vilmar Dorner, 49, is still unaccounted for.
The Coast Guard suspended the search for the missing boater on Saturday after an unsuccessful search with State Police.

title: After officiating Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding, Adam Sandler played pickup basketball in this Mass. vacation hotspot
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Adam Sandler officiated the wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce at Madison Square Garden on Friday.
Two days later, Sandler played pickup basketball with locals on Nantucket after seeking game locations via non-emergency police line.
Sandler impressed players with his passing skills and was described as a 'good dude' by those who posed for photos with him.

title: Local obituary: Oliver Lewis, 15, ‘an old soul, with wisdom far beyond his years’
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Oliver Lewis, 15, of Franklin, Massachusetts, died on June 23, 2026, from a bicycle accident.
He was known for his kindness, passion for reading, and involvement in school sports, excelling academically.
A scholarship fund at Bishop Feehan High School honors his generous spirit, supporting students in financial need.
